text: Judging from the literature, it appears that many researchers discuss the methods and strategies that are used by teachers to build the religious character of elementary school students. Based on the background of the problem, this study focuses on how Islamic Religious Education (PAI) teachers at SDN 2 Kesumadadi make the students build their religious character by cultivating them through the Asma’ al-Husna (the 99 Names of Allah). Although the curriculum is only 4 names of Allah, the teachers extend the names to 99 in order to nourish the students’ souls and to reduce the fear that faced by students when dealing with Arabic. In this study, the researcher uses qualitative field research methods to gather the data by doing direct observation, deep interview, and documentation. The results show that the Islamic Religious Education teachers at the elementary school implement the collective recitation of Asma’ al-Husna within the religious education class by using a strategy that is built upon five aspects, namely uswah hasanah (good examples), habituation, mentoring (by using the Latin transliteration to replace the Arabic script), appreciating, and punishing students. The purpose of the strategy is to inculcate Islamic values to the students in order to develop their spiritual and emotional intelligence in the elementary school context.
explanation: The piece shows domain-specific Indonesian educational context with some awkward English phrasing and plausible research details that suggest human authorship; while AI could imitate this, the nuance and minor inconsistencies point away from high-probability AI generation.
